subject: [PATCH v6 0/2] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that __filemap_get_folio_mpol() uses atomic folio_test_clear_dropbehind(), but the patch description does not mention any changes to this function, and requested a clarification on whether this change is intended
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, good catch with the folio_test_clear_dropbehind(). Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara noted that the patch series is generally good and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag from himself.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-05-06.patch_summary: This patch series improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by addressing several bugs and optimizing the handling of dirty pages. The changes include tracking DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kicking the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and adding a mechanism to run 'perf lock' around testcases. Additionally, the patch series includes several performance optimizations, such as using atomic operations and moving function calls outside locked sections to prevent lost wakeups and spin_unlock_irq() re-enabling interrupts while holding the i_pages xa_lock.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Andrew Morton expressed disappointment and frustration with the quality of the patch series, suggesting that it is not up to par compared to previous versions, and referencing a separate patchset by Sashiko that has found additional issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Geeze, is that the best you can do ;)

Sashiko seems to have found more stuff:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260505-dontcache-v6-0-66463805dd6a@kernel.org

2026-05-08.patch_summary: This patch series improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by addressing several bugs and optimizing the handling of dirty pages. The changes include tracking DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kicking the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and adding a mechanism to run 'perf lock' around testcases. Additionally, the patch series includes several performance optimizations, such as using atomic operations and moving function calls outside locked sections to prevent lost wakeups and spin_unlock_irq() re-enabling interrupts while holding the i_pages xa_lock.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that there were additional issues to address, confirmed they are working on fixes and will post a new version (v7) early next week.
reviews.raw_body:
I saw those after I sent the last set and have been working on
addressing them. I've also found a couple more via dueling Gemini and
Claude reviews.

I'll have a v7 posting coming early next week.

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
